input.description: Check for plan file and evidence dir

data.output:
1	# Story 9 — Agent Availability Tab — Final Plan (Reconciled)
2	
3	## Diagnosis
4	
5	Most data plane already exists. Story 9 is mostly UI labeling, model-inventory exposure, and cross-link plumbing — not new infrastructure.
6	
7	**Already shipped:**
8	- CLI: `ddx agent providers/models/capabilities/check/doctor/route-status`.
9	- HTTP: `/api/agent/models`, `/api/agent/catalog`, `/api/agent/capabilities`.
10	- GraphQL: `providerStatuses`, `harnessStatuses` (unified `ProviderStatus`: kind, reachable, cooldownUntil, lastCheckedAt, modelCount, usage, quota, sparkline, defaultForProfile), `defaultRouteStatus`, `providerTrend`.
11	- Frontend: `/nodes/[nodeId]/providers` table + drilldown, 2.5s polling, async probe refresh via `refreshProviderStatuses`.
12	
13	**Real gap (codex-confirmed):** model **identifiers** per provider are invisible in UI; `ProviderStatus.modelCount` is a number only. HTTP `/api/agent/models?provider=X` exists but is not surfaced in GraphQL or the page.
14	
15	**Codex corrections folded in:**
16	- `discoveredModels` must NOT be in the 2.5s-polled table query directly — `fizeau.ListModels` does live discovery; would hammer OpenRouter/LM Studio. Use a separate `providerModels(name, kind)` query OR a cached field with explicit refresh; respect existing in-flight guards in `resolver_providers.go`.
17	- Manual refresh must reuse the same status/model cache path, not fight the existing async refresher.
18	- Multi-node aggregation (`@vidar`/`@bragi` as separate ddx-servers) stays deferred per FEAT-021/SD-021. Story 9 ships single-server multi-endpoint only. Doc note, not code.
19	- Drop new `recentRuns()` query; instead extend `agentSessions` with `provider`/`harness`/`model` filters (sessions index already exists; `AgentSession` currently exposes only harness/model — add provider).
20	- Story 8 worker→provider links split out — they depend on Story 8 UI; recent-usage from sessions does NOT.
21	- Add route/tier/power visibility: `WorkerExecutionResult.Tier` exists in Go (`workers.go:631`) but isn't in GraphQL schema (`schema.graphql:591`) — expose it.
22	- Sanitize `ProviderStatus.baseURL` (strip userinfo/query params) before exposing.
23	- Auth: any new mutation must stay behind `requireTrusted` (`server.go:561`).
24	- Don't AC against `defaultRouteStatus.strategy` — resolver doesn't populate it (`resolver_providers.go:141`); either fix or omit from ACs.
25	- Drop the `<type>-<host>` naming TD; it's convention, not architecture.
26	
27	## Acceptance Criteria
28	
29	1. Tab labeled "Agent availability" reachable from main nav in one click.
30	2. With config `claude` + `codex` (harnesses) + `openrouter` + `lmstudio-vidar` + `lmstudio-bragi` (endpoints), all 5 rows render with kind, reachable status, default-marker, last-checked time, sanitized baseURL.
31	3. Each row shows model count; expand or drilldown lists discovered model IDs (cached; not live-probed every poll).
32	4. Manual "refresh models" action invalidates the model cache and re-probes; row updates within 5s; errors inline; reuses existing in-flight guards.
33	5. Provider drilldown shows "Recent usage" — last N sessions that used this provider/harness/model (via extended `agentSessions` filter), each linking to execution detail.
34	6. Worker rows on Story 8 page link to provider drilldown (deferred until Story 8 UI lands).
35	7. Worker / recent-usage entries display requested route key or tier/power bounds and the selected provider/model when recorded.
36	8. Unreachable providers render with status + error from `detail`; manual refresh re-probes.
37	9. `defaultRouteStatus` widget continues to show current auto-pick (no new dependency on `strategy` field).
38	
39	## Bead Breakdown (reordered per codex)
40	
41	1. **B9.1 — Model inventory: cached `providerModels(name, kind)` query + model cache + `refreshProviderModels` mutation**
42	   - Deps: none.
43	   - AC: query returns model IDs for endpoint and harness kinds from cache; mutation invalidates and re-probes; mutation gated by `requireTrusted`; in-flight guard prevents pile-on; `lastModelRefreshAt` + `modelRefreshStatus/error` exposed; `baseURL` sanitized in `ProviderStatus`.
44	   - Tier: medium.
45	
46	2. **B9.2 — Provider UI: rename to "Agent availability" + nav placement + model list inline/drilldown + manual refresh button**
47	   - Deps: B9.1.
48	   - AC: header renamed; tab in nav; rows show up to 5 models inline (or "+N more"); drilldown lists all models with context window when known; refresh button wired to mutation.
49	   - Tier: small.
50	
51	3. **B9.3 — e2e for the exact 5-provider scenario**
52	   - Deps: B9.2.
53	   - AC: Playwright fixture with claude + codex + openrouter + mock lmstudio-vidar + mock lmstudio-bragi; all 5 rows visible; model lists present; drilldown opens; refresh works.
54	   - Tier: small.
55	
56	4. **B9.4 — Recent usage on provider drilldown via extended `agentSessions` filters**
57	   - Deps: B9.2.
58	   - AC: `agentSessions` accepts `provider`/`harness`/`model` filters; `AgentSession` exposes `provider`; drilldown lists last 10 sessions with execution-detail links; tier/power/route key shown when recorded (requires exposing `WorkerExecutionResult.Tier` in GraphQL).
59	   - Tier: medium.
60	
61	5. **B9.5 — Story 8 worker → provider bidirectional link**
62	   - Deps: Story 8 workers UI merged + B9.2.
63	   - AC: worker rows wrap provider/model cells in `<a href="/nodes/.../providers/{name}">`; reverse listing already covered by B9.4.
64	   - Tier: small.
65	
66	(Optional B9.6 — file follow-up FEAT for true multi-node federation; not in this story.)
67	
68	## Tier Suggestions
69	
70	- Cheap-tier do (qwen-coder etc.): B9.2 (mostly Svelte + wiring), B9.3 (Playwright fixture + assertions), B9.5 (small UI link work).
71	- Strong-tier do or review: B9.1 (cache semantics, in-flight guards, mutation auth, baseURL sanitization — easy to get wrong), B9.4 (schema extension on `agentSessions` + `AgentSession.provider` + tier exposure — touches multiple resolvers).
72	- Deterministic top-of-ladder: schema regen, gofmt, golangci-lint, `go test ./cmd`, `bun run test`, Playwright.
73	
74	## Flagged Ambiguities
75	
76	1. "Usage" — tokens/requests (already shown) vs cost rollup (not shown). Codex flagged. Default to existing tokens/requests; add cost only if user confirms.
77	2. `defaultRouteStatus.strategy` not populated by resolver — either fix in B9.1 or drop from any AC. Defaulting to drop.
78	3. LM Studio model cache TTL — codex didn't pin a value. Suggest 60s default + manual refresh; confirm before implementing.
79	4. Whether `AgentSession.provider` requires backfilling historical sessions or is forward-only. Default forward-only.
80	5. Exact nav placement of the renamed tab — depends on current NavShell layout decisions from sibling stories.
81	
82	## Coordination Notes (sibling stories)
83	
84	- **Story 8 (workers tab):** B9.5 hard-depends on it. B9.4 supersedes part of original "recent runs" by sourcing from sessions, so Story 9 is no longer blocked on Story 8 for the drilldown.
85	- **Story 7-ish (NavShell / tab labels):** rename in B9.2 must coordinate with whoever owns top-level nav inventory; avoid double-rename collisions.
86	- **FEAT-014 (token awareness):** no change; usage already wired.
87	- **FEAT-021/SD-021 (multi-node):** add a doc note in PRD-level Story-9 doc that `@vidar`/`@bragi` is single-server multi-endpoint for now; true federation deferred.
88	- **WorkerExecutionResult.Tier exposure** in B9.4 may also benefit Story 8 — coordinate so both stories don't add the field twice.
89

data.text: The parent epic's only acceptance is "All child beads closed" — and all 5 children (B9.1–B9.5) are still open. Nothing for this attempt to implement; this bead just tracks completion of its children.

input.content:
Bead ddx-12e97cb9 is the Story 9 epic. Its sole acceptance criterion is
"All child beads closed." It has no implementation work of its own; it is a
tracking container for B9.1–B9.5.

Current child status (all open):
  - ddx-791a4f65  B9.1  cached providerModels query + refreshProviderModels mutation
  - ddx-bbac06c9  B9.2  rename to 'Agent availability' + nav + model list + refresh
  - ddx-1c8719eb  B9.3  Playwright e2e for the 5-provider scenario
  - ddx-1bca5898  B9.4  recent usage on drilldown + AgentSession.provider + Tier
  - ddx-99419bc1  B9.5  Story 8 worker→provider links

What is done: nothing in this attempt; nothing is required of the epic itself.

What is blocking: the five child beads must close before the epic's AC can be
satisfied. Each child carries its own testable ACs per /tmp/story-9-final.md.

What a follow-up attempt would need: re-run this bead only after all five
children are closed. At that point the epic can be closed without code changes.

No source files were modified. No commit produced.

data.text: No code changes. The epic ddx-12e97cb9 is a tracking container whose only AC is "all child beads closed"; its 5 children (B9.1–B9.5) are all still open, so the epic cannot close in this attempt. Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child status and exit conditions.
